title: External transmission data protection monitoring

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for 6.4.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SWIFT CSP 6.4 (Logging and monitoring) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.34.7: External transmission data protection monitoring.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.34.7' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: SWIFT Alliance/Lite2 transaction logs, operator session audit trails, network infrastructure logs, endpoint security logs, privileged access management, change management records, vulnerability scan outputs, multi-factor authentication logs
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=network sourcetype="pan:traffic" OR index=ssl sourcetype="stream:tls" earliest=-24h
| stats dc(cipher) as ciphers, values(ssl_version) as tls by dest_ip, dest_port
| where dest_port=443 AND mvfind(tls,"TLS1_0")>=0
| lookup swift_external_endpoints.csv dest_ip OUTPUT approved
| where approved!="true"
description: Produces SWIFT Customer Security Programme evidence for external transmission data protection monitoring ahead of independent assessment.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SWIFT CSP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes network, ssl and sourcetypes pan:traffic, stream:tls. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Segment SWIFT CSP evidence into dedicated indexes with strict role-based access. (2) Join operators to HR screening and vendor risk lookups refreshed weekly. (3) Validate MFA and PAM sourcetypes are CIM-mapped for Authentication models. (4) Retain evidence for at least 13 months aligned to attestation cycles. Indexes required: `index=network`, `index=ssl`.
visualization: Table (operators), Single value (sessions), Bar chart (CSCF controls). Key fields: `ciphers`, `dest_ip`, `dest_port`, `tls`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Operator sessions from authorized payment operations, scheduled message corrections, or vendor-supervised maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: SWIFT Alliance/Lite2 transaction logs, operator session audit trails, network infrastructure logs, endpoint security logs, privileged access management, change management records, vulnerability scan outputs, multi-factor authentication logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Segment SWIFT CSP evidence into dedicated indexes with strict role-based access. (2) Join operators to HR screening and vendor risk lookups refreshed weekly. (3) Validate MFA and PAM sourcetypes are CIM-mapped for Authentication models. (4) Retain evidence for at least 13 months aligned to attestation cycles.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype="pan:traffic" OR index=ssl sourcetype="stream:tls" earliest=-24h
| stats dc(cipher) as ciphers, values(ssl_version) as tls by dest_ip, dest_port
| where dest_port=443 AND mvfind(tls,"TLS1_0")>=0
| lookup swift_external_endpoints.csv dest_ip OUTPUT approved
| where approved!="true"
```

Understanding this SPL

**External transmission data protection monitoring (SWIFT CSCF advisory)** — Produces SWIFT Customer Security Programme evidence for external transmission data protection monitoring ahead of independent assessment.

Documented **Data sources**: SWIFT Alliance/Lite2 transaction logs, operator session audit trails, network infrastructure logs, endpoint security logs, privileged access management, change management records, vulnerability scan outputs, multi-factor authentication logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network, ssl; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic, stream:tls. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, index=ssl, sourcetype="pan:traffic", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by dest_ip, dest_port** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where dest_port=443 AND mvfind(tls,"TLS1_0")>=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where approved!="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**External transmission data protection monitoring (SWIFT CSCF advisory)** — Produces SWIFT Customer Security Programme evidence for external transmission data protection monitoring ahead of independent assessment.

Documented **Data sources**: SWIFT Alliance/Lite2 transaction logs, operator session audit trails, network infrastructure logs, endpoint security logs, privileged access management, change management records, vulnerability scan outputs, multi-factor authentication logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the audit register; for access or operator activity, also compare with the source-of-truth for SWIFT application sessions in your environment.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (operators), Single value (sessions), Bar chart (CSCF controls).
grandmaExplanation: We help you show SWIFT security programme expectations are met for your payment zone, operators, and controls before the independent check.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Authentication (CIM data model).
